title: Venture Pro iQube (v1.0)
description: PREMIUM, CUMULATIVE upload schema for Venture Lab Lite/Pro/Elite. It is a strict SUPERSET of the Venture Light schema (v0.5): it carries everything Light does (operator, strategy, experienceModel, experienceGuide, standing, plan) PLUS the full 13-layer VentureQube per venture. A premium operator downloads and maintains ONLY this one file — they never also need the Light schema.

_agent_briefing.audience: An off-platform AI assistant helping a premium operator prepare a Venture Pro iQube to upload into their aigentMe / Venture Lab.
_agent_briefing.cumulative_design: Schemas are cumulative. Venture Light (v0.5) is the base. Venture Pro (this file, v1.0) = Light + the full per-venture 13-layer VentureQube. Venture Portfolio = Pro + multiple ventures + a portfolio block. So each tier's download is a superset of the tier below; a Pro user uploads this single file to populate ExperienceModel, ExperienceGuide, Standing AND the full venture blueprint.
_agent_briefing.what_to_produce: A single JSON file conforming to this schema. Fill the base blocks (operator/strategy/experienceModel/experienceGuide/standing/plan) exactly as in Venture Light, then fill each venture's 13 layers. Use concrete, verifiable content; leave a layer's optional fields out rather than inventing them.
the_13_layers: 2 thesis — mission, vision, problem, value proposition, market/industry tags
4 signalEvidence — demand/capability/opportunity signals (confidence is Standing-calibrated server-side; you may leave confidences null)
5 archetypes — customer kinds, pains, willingness to pay
9 resource — people, agents, tools, iqubes, capital
10 execution — phases with objectives, deliverables, success metrics
11 delegation — which agents (aigentMe, devon, marketa, venture-lab, investor-office) execute what
12 outcome — outcomes, proofOfOutcomeClaims (verification-gated Net Value Acceleration evidence), standing changes, lessons
13 governance — risk/sensitivity/accuracy/verifiability + ventureConfidence (mostly server-calibrated; leave null)
14 institutional — venture-lab/investment status, commons + public visibility, readiness
_agent_briefing.calibration_note: signalEvidence.* and governance.* confidence scores are calibrated by Standing + metaCommons signals on the server; you may set them or leave them null. capability.availableCapabilities is auto-filled from the operator's Standing — leave it empty unless the operator wants to override.
_agent_briefing.knowledge_artifact_vs_operating_artifact: There are two distinct concerns and you must not collapse them. The CORE VentureQube (operator/strategy/experience/standing/ventures) is a KNOWLEDGE ARTIFACT — a general-purpose primitive that a student, founder, creator, venture studio, family office, or polity steward can all use unchanged. Do NOT hardwire daily actions / campaigns / commercial-operating goals into it. The OPTIONAL top-level `operatingModel` block is the OPERATING ARTIFACT — the living brief aigentMe runs as Chief of Staff (mission, successMetrics, activeObjectives, priorityPartners, priorityActions, reviewCadence, primaryMetric). Three concerns, three homes: Experience captures intent; Ventures capture execution structure; operatingModel captures action. Fill operatingModel only if the operator wants aigentMe operating against a daily brief; otherwise omit it.

experienceGuide.description: Same as Venture Light (v0.5): sphereMaturity / sphereAlignment (energy, body, mind, emotion, relationship, community, legacy), precedenceMode, focusIntent.

standing.description: Same as Venture Light (v0.5): standing.core (whoYouAre, whatYouKnow, whatYouveDone, interests, intentions, experiencesThatMatter, accomplish) + optional linkedInUrl.

plan.description: Same as Venture Light — horizons today/next24h/next7d/next30d/next90d, each { focus, actions[] }.

kpiBoard.description: Same as Venture Light — cross-venture KPI rows that hydrate the cockpit's Active KPIs.

operatingModel.description: OPTIONAL operating-artifact layer (Pro/Portfolio only). The living operational brief aigentMe executes against as your Chief of Staff. This is the ONE place day-to-day operations live — the core VentureQube stays a general-purpose primitive (intent in Experience, structure in Ventures); operations live here. Leave it out entirely if you only want the knowledge artifact.

mission.description: The OPERATIONAL expression of your strategy/portfolio thesis — what you are doing right now in service of that Why (e.g. "Operation Leap"). Do NOT write a second thesis here; the strategy/portfolio thesis is the single Why.

successMetrics.description: What success looks like, concretely — e.g. "4,000 Passport holders", "$100K MRR", "25 Founder Office conversions". These are outcomes.

activeObjectives.description: Objectives currently in flight, each with a lifecycle status so aigentMe knows what to act on.

priorityPartners.description: Strategic partners this operating brief depends on.

priorityActions.description: The next concrete actions, highest-priority first.

nextReviewDate.description: When the next review is due (ISO date). Cadence is how often; this is when — lets aigentMe auto-generate the next review brief.

primaryMetric.description: The single north-star KPI. Layered framing: public mental model = Time-to-Value; internal metric = Proof of Time Saved (PoTS); constitutional principle = Net Value Acceleration (Time-to-Value minus Risk Repair Burden), which rolls up every venture's verified outcome accrual. Net Value Acceleration is the default.

ventures.description: Pro ventures — each carries the full 13-layer VentureQube. (Lite tier: 1 venture; Pro: up to 3; Elite: unlimited — enforced server-side.)

confidence.description: 0–100, or null to let Standing/metaCommons calibrate it server-side.

availableCapabilities.description: Leave empty — auto-filled from the operator's Standing.

proofOfOutcomeClaims.description: Verification-gated Outcome Accrual Evidence. Each claim is self-declared (verificationStatus 'claimed') and accrues NOTHING to Standing until a verifier moves it to 'verified'. Net Value Acceleration = max(0, timeSavedHours − riskRepairHours); Standing accrual = NVA × confidence, once.

riskRepairHours.description: Time spent repairing risk the venture introduced (subtracted).

accruedAt.description: Server-set once accrued — leave unset on upload.

governance.description: Mostly server-calibrated — leave null unless overriding.
